sources.title_original: Superconducting Motor with Spoke-Supported Rotor Windings
sources.title_en: Superconducting Motor with Spoke-Supported Rotor Windings
sources.relevance: Anchor exemplar's own patent: rotating cryocooler cold end through hollow driveshaft with radial thermally conductive straps to rotor shell.

sources.assignee: unconfirmed (likely Hinetics, claim language matches T1-01 family)
sources.title_original: Superconducting motor with reduced coil stress
sources.title_en: Superconducting motor with reduced coil stress
sources.relevance: Flexible radial thermal straps accommodating cooldown thermal contraction on same rotating radial-strap heat path as T1-01.

sources.title_original: 旋转参考系中的冷却系统
sources.title_en: Cooling system in a rotating reference frame
sources.relevance: Mounts cryocooler cold head in the rotating frame itself to avoid cryogenic rotary joints; direct alternate mechanism to CF-5.

sources.title_original: Supraleitende Einrichtung mit einer Kuehleinheit fuer eine rotierende supraleitende Wicklung
sources.title_en: Superconducting device with a cooling unit for a rotating superconducting coil
sources.relevance: Cold head rotates inside rotor, stationary compressor, ferrofluid gas coupling as sole mechanical connection across interface.

sources.assignee: American Superconductor Corp / Reliance Electric Co
sources.title_original: Superconductor rotor cooling system
sources.relevance: Stationary cryocooler bridged to rotating device via interleaved stationary/rotating copper fins across a gap; no fluid rotary seal.

sources.title_original: Cooling system for HTS machines
sources.relevance: High-speed fans (10-30k rpm) circulate helium in rotating frame via density-driven centrifugal circulation, ferrofluidic rotary seals to stationary cryocoolers.

sources.title_original: HTS superconducting rotating machine
sources.relevance: Explicitly teaches keeping cryocooler stationary to avoid gravity-heat-transfer problems of a rotating cold head; rotary coupling for closed rotating loop.

sources.title_original: Cryocooler and rotary joint
sources.relevance: Compressor stationary/expander rotating with staged intermediate-pressure working-gas seal; applied to superconducting wind-power generator rotor.

sources.assignee: Tokyo University of Marine Science and Technology (NUC) / Kitano Seiki Co., Ltd.
sources.title_original: Cryo-rotary joint
sources.relevance: Compact cryo-rotary joint with magnetic-fluid seal positioned to minimize parasitic heat leak into refrigerant path.

sources.title_original: Cooling structure of superconducting motor
sources.relevance: LN2 grooves cut into outer surface of rotating shaft (not center-drilled) to route coolant closer to coil; cryogen-based not conduction-cooled, but relevant shaft-routing geometry precedent.

sources.title_original: 一种基于传导冷却的超导电机的制冷系统
sources.title_en: A conduction-cooling-based superconducting motor refrigeration system
sources.relevance: Rotating two-stage cold head + copper/aluminum thermal straps to SC magnet, rotary seal for helium supply, targeted at <20rpm wind gen -- near-identical mechanism to a rotating-cold-head embodiment of CF-5. HIGH PRIORITY, hurts candidate.

sources.assignee: Hyundai Heavy Industries Co., Ltd. (now HD Hyundai Heavy Industries)
sources.title_original: 전도 냉각 방식 초전도 회전기
sources.relevance: 3-stage GM cryocooler mounted inside rotor, circumferential shaft holes + copper elements shorten conduction path to SC wire -- near-identical core mechanism to CF-5, filed a decade before Hinetics. HIGH PRIORITY, hurts candidate.

sources.assignee: likely Hyundai Heavy Industries (unconfirmed, companion to T1-12)
sources.title_original: 초전도 회전기용 다단 구조를 갖는 자성유체 실링 장치
sources.title_en: Multi-stage magnetic-fluid sealing device for a superconducting rotating machine
sources.relevance: Multi-stage ferrofluidic seal for hollow rotating shaft housing a cold rod, adjacent rotary-seal detail to T1-12's mechanism.

sources.title_original: Synchronous machine having cryogenic gas transfer coupling to rotor with super-conducting coils
sources.relevance: Cryogenic gas transfer joint from stationary cryorefrigerator through stationary bayonet into rotating tube; alternate transfer-across-interface mechanism.

sources.title_original: Cryogenic rotary transfer coupling for superconducting electromechanical machine
sources.relevance: Dedicated cryogenic rotary transfer coupling patent, core rotary-interface hardware in CF-5's problem space.

sources.title_original: Superconducting rotor for an electrical machine
sources.relevance: Early-generation superconducting rotor general architecture background art.

sources.title_original: Superconducting rotor
sources.relevance: General superconducting rotor background art, pre-cryocooler-conduction era.

sources.title_original: Cryogenic cooling system for rotor having a high temperature super-conducting field winding
sources.relevance: Evaporative cryogen cooling with recondensing cryorefrigerator; hybrid cryogen-bath-plus-cryocooler bracketing background art.

sources.title_original: Superconducting rotor with cooling system
sources.relevance: Addresses complexity of multi-cylindrical cryogenic flow-channel rotor structures; simplification-adjacent background art.

sources.title_original: High-temperature superconducting rotating machine equipped with fixed-type rotor cryostat for cryogen and stator cooling structure using of vaporized cryogen from rotor
sources.relevance: Fixed (non-rotating) rotor cryostat using boiled-off cryogen to also cool stator; adjacent but structurally distinct, cryogen-bath-based. Needs manual re-pull (image-PDF blocked parsing).

sources.title_original: 도전성 물질로 함침된 무절연 초전도 코일 및 그의 제조장치
sources.title_en: No-insulation superconducting coil impregnated with conductive material, and manufacturing apparatus therefor
sources.relevance: NI coil thermal/electrical interface engineering; not rotating-frame-specific but touches NI-half of CF-5's compound claim scope.

sources.venue: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics, vol. 58, no. 8
sources.title_original: A Spoke-Supported Superconducting Rotor With Rotating Cryocooler
sources.relevance: Academic parent publication of Hinetics T1-01/T1-02 patent family; useful for enablement and public-disclosure-date checks.

sources.title_original: Rotating Cryocooler Performance for Superconducting Rotor
sources.relevance: Follow-on experimental characterization of rotating-cryocooler performance, same architecture family as T3-01/T1-01.

sources.title_original: Design and Analysis of Rotor Structure Support for Spoke Type Superconducting Motor
sources.relevance: Further structural follow-on in spoke-supported rotor family; background/enablement art.

sources.title_original: High Temperature Superconducting Motor Cooled by On-Board Cryocooler
sources.relevance: Earlier-generation on-board rotating cryocooler HTS motor demonstration, predates Hinetics by over a decade.

sources.title_original: Rotating Cryocooler for Superconducting Motor
sources.relevance: Stirling coaxial pulse-tube cryocooler mounted in physical/thermal contact with HTS rotor, rotated together on 1800 rpm mock-up; early demonstrated rotating-cold-head mechanism.

sources.title_original: Cryogenic Rotary Joints Applied to the Cooling of Superconducting Rotating Machinery
sources.relevance: Introduces Gas Blow Coupling (GBC), non-contact thermal coupling via fluid medium across narrow gap; strong different-mechanism NPL reference for G-CLAIM design-around.

sources.title_original: Noncontact High-Torque Magnetic Coupler for Superconducting Rotating Machines
sources.relevance: Non-contact torque transfer isolating cold rotating mass from warm shaft; adjacent mechanical-isolation background art.

sources.title_original: Cooling of Superconducting Motors on Aircraft
sources.relevance: Review-level survey of aviation HTS motor cooling incl. rotor cryocooling; full text blocked (HTTP 403), retried once, captured from snippet only.

sources.title_original: Cooling method for the rotor of a superconducting motor
sources.relevance: Directly on-topic title; full text blocked (HTTP 403), retried once, content unverified.

sources.title_original: Additive manufacturing materials for structural optimisation and cooling enhancement of superconducting motors in cryo-electric aircraft
sources.relevance: Adjacent structural/thermal-materials background for cryo-electric aircraft motors, not rotating-interface-specific.

sources.title_original: Cooling System Technologies on Superconducting Rotating Machines / Rotating Vacuum Heat Transfer, Rotating Cryocoolers, Slip Rings, Rotating Bearings, Ball Bearings (book chapters)
sources.relevance: Dedicated 2024 book with chapters titled almost exactly on CF-5's problem statement; likely richest NPL survey source. Not independently pulled past title/TOC (paywalled).

sources.title_original: 转子、电机和用电设备
sources.relevance: Conventional (non-superconducting) EV motor rotor cooling channel patent; keyword-overlap noise hit, excluded from IDS_pool as off-scope.

gaps_logged: No native authenticated queries run against CNIPA, J-PlatPat, KIPRIS, or Espacenet/WIPO PatentScope consoles -- all non-English hits surfaced via Google Patents aggregated mirror + Google web search, cross-checked on Google Patents record pages.
US12531457 and US12494701 PDFs were image-only OCR content; WebFetch could not parse text -- bibliographic data partial, flagged for manual re-pull.
Two IOPscience article pages (T3-09, T3-10) returned HTTP 403 on WebFetch; retried once per protocol, still blocked; captured from search-snippet metadata only.
GE Aviation / GE Global Research assignee-specific rotor-cryocooler filings not independently isolated from AMSC/Reliance Electric family this pass -- dedicated re-run recommended.
Several patent assignees (T1-04, T1-13 through T1-17, T2-01, T2-02) not independently confirmed via primary assignment records -- flagged low/medium confidence pending verification.
